Records.authors: Piotr Bargiela, Fabrizio Caola, Andreas von Manteuffel, Lorenzo Tancredi
Records.description: The three-loop four-point massless Master Integrals are computed in planar, non-planar, and double-non-planar sectors. The result is given in terms of Harmonic Polylogarithms.

Three-loop massless four-point 'XX-box' topology with 28 master integrals (Fig. 2), reduced via an experimental version of LiteRed. Differential equations in the dimensionless variable $x = t/s$ are obtained for 25 of the 28 masters (the 3 top-sector integrals are excluded due to IBP-reduction complexity). The system has Fuchsian singular points at $x = 0, -1, \infty$ (i.e. $t=0, u=0, s=0$). The paper demonstrates the reduction-to-epsilon-form algorithm explicitly on the $3\times 3$ diagonal block spanned by indices $\{23,24,25\}$ (Eq. 8.2 onward), and reports that all diagonal blocks are similarly brought to epsilon-form, after which the off-diagonal pieces are removed by the block-triangular procedure of Section 7. The full M-matrix is not printed.

Records.authors: Henn, Johannes, Mistlberger, Bernhard, Smirnov, Vladimir A., Wasser, Pascal
Records.description: Three-loop non-planar box family D. Double-crossed non-planar topology with crossings on both sides.

Records.authors: Gardi, Einan; Herzog, Franz; Jones, Stephen; Ma, Yao
Records.description: Mandelstam's graph G_tt: a 10-propagator three-loop massless 2->2 nonplanar graph analysed in the Regge limit. The terms proportional to s12 in its Symanzik F are identical to those of the crown G_bullet_bullet; it has a unique hidden (Glauber) region but, unlike the crown, the hidden region is NOT leading (the leading regions are facet regions with collinear*soft modes). The paper defines G_tt via its figure and Symanzik polynomials only (no momentum-space propagator denominator list is printed), so the propagators field abstains per spec §4.2; the graph (edges) was reconstructed from the figure and confirmed by computeNickelIndex. Belongs to integral family 1(d) of Henn:2020lye.
